name: Turn-of-The-Century Molasses Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F with the rack in the center.
2. Generously grease a 12-cup-capacity bundt pan. Lightly dust with flour, tapping the pan over the sink to remove excess flour.
3. Combine the molasses and baking soda; set aside.
4. Using a mixer, cream together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
5. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
6. Incorporate the reserved molasses mixture.
7. Sift in the flour and salt, then alternate with milk until combined and smooth.
8. Transfer the batter to the prepared pan and bake until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, about 50 minutes.
9. Set the pan on a cooling rack; let cool for 15 minutes.
10. Gently invert the pan and remove the cake. Let it rest at least 45 minutes before serving.
11. Serve warm. The cake can be made up to 3 months in advance and kept at room temperature, well-covered, or frozen tightly wrapped. To serve, thaw the cake first if frozen, then reheat it in an oven set to 350°F until warmed through for about 15 minutes (or alternatively, in a microwave on medium power until just warmed through, not hot). Gently sift confectioners' sugar over the top before serving.
